notes: Tier 1.5 physics engine — ROBUST champions optimised for manufacturing yield, not peak performance. Each entry is the Pareto-optimal point that maximises MC yield while retaining competitive metrics. High-performance alternatives documented in notes fields. EM d33 is phi-calibrated (+1.5 pC/N, literature-calibrated 2026-02-24). L/PM/E remain uncalibrated (phi=0).

L.notes: Robust champion. Ga_cation=27% (well above 20% minimum), cryst_risk=0.18 (comfortable margin below 0.50 threshold). MC yield 98.3% at +-3% sigma. High-performance alternative: In=0.253, Ga=0.072 (cryst=0.30, mu=42.3) scores higher but sits at cryst penalty boundary — not recommended for first fab run.

PM.phi_calibration_notes: delta_n phi offset +0.343 applied. Calibrated against Delaney 2020 (Adv. Funct. Mater.) for pure Sb2Se3: model=0.422 vs literature=0.765 at 1550 nm (-44.8% systematic underprediction). Model structural limitation: CM element mixing does not reconstruct Sb2Se3 compound polarisability. loss_k NOT corrected (literature k < 1e-5 is below detection limit; no reliable estimate for doped champion). Uncertainty: ±50% (1 calibration point, structural bias, no champion-specific literature). Update when >=3 ellipsometry measurements of champion film available.
PM.notes: Reference champion restored after GA yield analysis. GA found Sb0.675:Se4.36:Ge0.078:Cl0.032 with FOM=1187 (+10%) but delta_n=0.301 (39% MC yield). Reference has delta_n=0.864 (phi-corrected; sim_raw=0.521, 188% margin above 0.30 threshold) → 100% MC yield. The Sb/delta_n trade-off is fundamental: reducing Sb improves FOM but pushes delta_n to the threshold. For lab manufacture, use Sb2:Se3:Ge:Cl. Research note: GA composition requires stoichiometry control to <1% on Sb. delta_n=0.864 and FOM=1789 are phi-corrected (±50% uncertainty; structural model limitation).

E.notes: Robust champion at 559°C anneal (down from 583°C). Pr cost: 18.94 vs 19.16 (-1.1%), but 24°C furnace margin gained. Model sensitivity: anneal_temp CRITICAL (0.59 uC/cm2 per 10°C). Above 580°C agglomeration risk rises sharply. Stick to 550-560°C target with +-10°C furnace tolerance.

EM.phi_calibration_notes: d33 phi offset +1.5 pC/N applied. Calibrated against 3 independent literature validation points (PMC9030765: x=0.20, 9.9 pC/N; PMC8585448: x=0.29, 12.6 pC/N; multi-group consensus: x=0.40, 19.2 pC/N), all standard reactive magnetron sputtering. Model underpredicts by 8.9% mean. Uncertainty ±30% (3 points only). Update when >=5 real measurements from actual deposition system available. Akiyama 2009 dual-reactive cosputtering (x=0.43, 27.6 pC/N) excluded: different technique.
EM.notes: Robust champion at x_Sc=0.38 (down from 0.42). d33 cost: 18.01 vs high-perf alternative (18.28 pC/N phi-corrected), but MC yield 76.6% vs 0.1% for x=0.42. Phase fully stable (x=0.38 < x_cliff=0.41 on Si). sec_phase_risk threshold: _sec_phase_risk <= 0.60 (above this secondary ScN/Sc2O3 formation very likely). Literature confirms secondary phase above ~40% Sc is a real fab risk. DO NOT use x=0.42 for lab manufacture — MC analysis shows it fails 99.9% of the time under normal Sc-target calibration variability. d33=18.01 is phi-corrected (sim_raw=16.51 + phi=+1.5). Calibration uncertainty: ±30%.

M.notes: Carried forward from v1.0. TRL 1 (conceptual). No physics upgrade planned.
